economic_notes: Entities include SLFN Enterprises, Swan Lake First Nation Housing Authority, Swan Lake First Nation Gaming Commission, Swan Lake First Nation Farm, 6226124 Manitoba Ltd., SLFN Property Management Ltd. Operations include VLT/gaming, gas bar (Four Corners), RV park/log cabins, farm, property management, fibre optics, wind farm, potato chip plant. COVID-19 noted to have impacted VLT revenues and cabin rentals.
notes: Fiscal year ends March 31, 2022 - this is a 2022 year-end statement, not 2024. Province is Manitoba (MB). Six fully consolidated subsidiaries. Total government transfers estimated as ISC ($15,661,343) + FNIHB ($3,727,003) + Provincial funding ($533,388) = $19,921,734. OSR = $33,540,647 - $19,921,734 = $13,618,913. Current liabilities = bank indebtedness $125,019 + accounts payable $579,424 = $704,443 (deferred revenue shown separately). Long-term debt current portion is $2,417,822. Payroll note shows total eligible salaries of $3,529,698 for ISC-funded employees only; total salaries per segment disclosure is $5,258,809. No schedule of remuneration provided. CMHC Rapid Housing Initiative of $1,459,387 recognized as revenue but this is recurring program funding. Two large RBC loans secured against Headingley building totalling approximately $17.4M are significant. Supporting schedules pages 24-112 were compiled (not audited).
